name: COLORECTAL CARCINOMA
definition: Lynch syndrome annular constriction ← tumor grows along lymphatic channels that parallel circular long-segment stricture without significant mucosal abnormality similar to linitis plastica variant of mucin-secreting adenocarcinoma HEREDITARY NONPOLYPOSIS COLORECTAL CANCER SYNDROME (HNPCC) autosomal dominant disorder with high incidence of colorectal cancers + increased

demographics.Incidence: 136,830 new cases annually with 50,310 deaths in USA (2014); 11% of all newly
demographics.Histo: > 50% of cells have a cell nucleus that is displaced to periphery by large amount of
demographics.Etiology: autosomal dominant abnormality of chromosome 2 with defect in DNA
demographics.Prevalence: 5-10% of patients with colon cancer; 5 x more common than familial

dominant_finding: concentric ~ 1 cm thickening of bowel wall
discriminating_features: "saddle lesion" = growth characteristics between polypoid mass + annular constricting
curvilinear / mottled calcifications are CHARACTERISTIC of mucinous adenocarcinoma
commonly polypoid lesions with chronic bleeding + intussusception

general: commonly polypoid lesions with chronic bleeding + intussusception
"saddle lesion" = growth characteristics between polypoid mass + annular constricting
curvilinear / mottled calcifications are CHARACTERISTIC of mucinous adenocarcinoma
marked asymmetric colonic wall thickening involving a short segment
low-density mass + low-density lymph nodes in mucinous adenocarcinoma (if > 50% of
abrupt change from normal to abnormal segment of colon
signs of Lnn involvement: single lymph node > 1 cm in diameter / cluster of ≥ 3 nodes < 1
MR: concentric ~ 1 cm thickening of bowel wall
marked wall thickening + mild pericolic infiltration
often elongated infiltrating mass > 5 cm in length mimicking underlying colonic
concentric rigid wall thickening of scirrhous / linitis plastica appearance
may have lung metastases without liver metastases
NO interruption of hyperechoic submucosa = tumor confined to mucosa + submucosa
NO interruption of hyperechoic serosa = tumor confined to rectal wall
break in outermost hyperechoic layer = tumor penetrates into perirectal fat
irregular serrated outer border of muscularis propria (pseudopodia through serosa)
hypoechoic perirectal lymph nodes ← tumor involvement

clinical.Cx: perforation in 0.2% (0.02% for BE); death in 1÷5,000 (1÷50,000 for BE)
clinical.Spread: peritoneal surfaces + lymph nodes (more readily); hepatic parenchymal metastases
clinical.Prognosis: poor ← aggressive tumor + frequent local recurrence
